questions.question: Explain the greedy choice property of Huffman coding algorithm.
keyPoints: Greedy choice: Always pick two nodes with minimum frequency
Local optimal choice leads to global optimal solution
Makes decisions at each step without reconsidering
Proof via exchange argument

questions.question: What is the difference between Shannon-Fano coding and Huffman coding?
keyPoints: Huffman uses bottom-up approach, Shannon-Fano is top-down
Huffman guarantees optimal prefix codes
Shannon-Fano may not be optimal
Huffman is more widely used in practice

questions.question: Define entropy and explain its significance in data compression.
keyPoints: Entropy H = Σ p(x)log₂(1/p(x))
Represents theoretical minimum average bits
Measures information content of source
Huffman coding approaches but cannot be less than entropy

questions.question: How does Huffman coding handle decoding? Explain with an example.
keyPoints: Start at root node
Read each bit: 0→left child, 1→right child
When leaf is reached, output corresponding character
Return to root and continue until all bits processed

questions.question: What is the optimal merge pattern problem? How is it solved using Huffman coding?
keyPoints: Problem: Merge n files with given sizes optimally
Solution: Use Huffman algorithm
Always merge two smallest files first
Minimizes total cost = sum of all merge operations

questions.question: Explain why Huffman coding is called a lossless compression technique.
keyPoints: No information is lost during compression
Original data can be exactly reconstructed from encoded data
Unlike lossy compression (JPEG, MP3)
Achieved through prefix-free codes

questions.question: Trace the Huffman coding algorithm for the following character frequencies: A:45, B:13, C:12, D:16, E:9, F:5. Show all steps and determine the codewords for each character.
keyPoints: Step 1: Initial queue - F(5), E(9), C(12), B(13), D(16), A(45)
Step 2: Merge F+E=14, remaining: C(12), B(13), D(16), A(45), new(14)
Step 3: Merge C+B=25, remaining: D(16), new(14), A(45), new(25)
Step 4: Merge new(14)+D=30, remaining: new(25), A(45), new(30)
Step 5: Merge new(25)+A=70, remaining: new(30), new(70)
Step 6: Merge new(30)+new(70)=100 (root)

questions.question: Prove that Huffman coding produces an optimal prefix code.
keyPoints: Define optimality: minimizes weighted path length
Base case: For 2 symbols, Huffman is optimal
Induction hypothesis: optimal for n-1 symbols
Inductive step: show optimal for n symbols using exchange argument
Exchange any optimal tree with Huffman tree at deepest leaves
Greedy choice property ensures local optimum = global optimum

questions.question: Explain the optimal merge pattern problem and solve it for files of sizes: 20, 15, 10, 5, 25. Show the merge tree and total cost.
keyPoints: Problem: Find minimum cost to merge n files into one
Always merge two smallest files first (greedy)
Step 1: Merge 5+10=15 (cost 15)
Step 2: Merge 15+15=30 (cost 30)
Step 3: Merge 20+25=45 (cost 45)
Step 4: Merge 30+45=75 (cost 75)
Shows Huffman algorithm solves this optimally

questions.question: Calculate the compression ratio achieved by Huffman coding for a message containing characters A,B,C,D with frequencies 50,30,15,5 respectively. Given fixed-length code uses 2 bits per character.
keyPoints: Fixed length: 2 bits × 100 = 200 bits for 100 chars
Build Huffman tree:
Codewords: A=0, B=10, C=110, D=111
Compressed bits = 1.75 × 100 = 175 bits
